title: paronychia capitata var. serpyllifolia
intro: Paronychia capitata var. serpyllifolia is a plant species in the four o'clock family (Nyctaginaceae) and is commonly known as the 'small-leaved paronychia' or 'small-leaved wild basil'. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. It has been found to have various health benefits, such as soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and treating respiratory issues like coughs and colds. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
